image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Objects resist changes in motion — that's why seatbelts matter.
Context visual: This resistance is known as inertia, the tendency of objects to keep doing what they're doing.
Escalation visual: When you're in a car and it suddenly stops, your body wants to keep moving. That's why seatbelts save lives.
Evidence visual: Inertia is everywhere — from riding a bike to playing sports. Don't underestimate its power!
Payoff visual: Understanding inertia helps you make safe choices every day, especially on the road.
Cta visual: Want to learn more surprising science? Hit that follow button!
